skill.name: seo
skill.description: Optimize for search engine visibility and ranking. Use when asked to "improve SEO", "optimize for search", "fix meta tags", "add structured data", "sitemap optimization", or "search engine optimization".
skill.summary: Technical SEO optimization guide based on Lighthouse audits and Google Search guidelines.
skill.author: addyosmani

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is pure markdown documentation containing code examples for SEO implementation (HTML meta tags, JSON-LD structured data, CSS rules, robots.txt). The static scanner misidentified markdown code block backticks as Ruby shell execution, example URLs as hardcoded network calls, relative documentation links as path traversal, and security header examples as weak cryptography. No executable code, no prompt injection attempts, no malicious patterns detected. The skill is safe for publication.

content.user_title: Optimize your website for search engines
content.value_statement: Poor search engine visibility reduces organic traffic and revenue. This skill provides technical SEO guidance based on Lighthouse audits and Google Search guidelines to improve your ranking.
actual_capabilities: Generate proper title tags, meta descriptions, and heading structures for any page
Create JSON-LD structured data for Organization, Article, Product, FAQ, and Breadcrumb schemas
Configure robots.txt, XML sitemaps, and canonical URLs correctly
Optimize image SEO with descriptive alt text, filenames, and lazy loading
Implement mobile SEO best practices including responsive viewports and tap targets
Set up international SEO with hreflang tags and language declarations
limitations: Cannot improve backlinks or domain authority, which require off-site strategies
Does not replace actual Lighthouse tool execution for measuring page performance
Content quality and relevance factors are outside the scope of this skill
Cannot directly submit sitemaps to Google Search Console or verify site ownership

use_cases.title: Launch a new website with proper SEO
use_cases.description: Generate all necessary SEO elements including meta tags, structured data, robots.txt, and sitemap configuration before going live.

use_cases.title: Audit and fix existing SEO issues
use_cases.description: Identify missing title tags, duplicate content, broken canonical URLs, and incomplete structured data on production websites.

use_cases.title: Implement structured data for rich results
use_cases.description: Create JSON-LD schemas for products, articles, and FAQs to enable rich snippets in Google search results.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce store owners seeking better search visibility

prompt_templates.title: Quick SEO check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the HTML of my homepage and identify missing or incorrect SEO elements like title tags, meta descriptions, and heading structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: A small business owner wants to check their website SEO.

prompt_templates.title: Generate meta tags
prompt_templates.prompt: Create optimized title tags and meta descriptions for a product page selling handmade ceramics. The primary keyword is handmade ceramic vase.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: An e-commerce merchant needs SEO content for a new product.

prompt_templates.title: Add structured data
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate JSON-LD structured data for a blog post about sustainable gardening tips, including author, publisher, article schema, and FAQ section.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: A content publisher wants rich results for their articles.

prompt_templates.title: Full SEO audit and fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete technical SEO audit of my website structure. Check robots.txt configuration, URL structure, internal linking patterns, canonical URL setup, and generate a prioritized fix list.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: A development team needs a comprehensive SEO review before launch.

output_examples.input: Generate a title tag and meta description for a bakery selling fresh sourdough bread.
output_examples.output:
Title tag: Fresh Sourdough Bread | Artisan Baked Daily | [Bakery Name]
Meta description: Order handcrafted sourdough bread baked fresh every morning. Made with organic flour and natural starter. Free local delivery. Order by 6 PM for next-day pickup.

output_examples.input: Create JSON-LD structured data for a local restaurant called Bella Italia at 123 Main Street, open Tuesday through Sunday.
output_examples.output: JSON-LD schema for LocalBusiness with Restaurant type, including name, address, opening hours, phone number, and aggregate rating markup.

best_practices: Use unique, descriptive title tags with primary keywords near the beginning for every page
Implement JSON-LD structured data following schema.org standards and validate with Google Rich Results Test
Set self-referencing canonical URLs on all pages to prevent duplicate content issues
anti_patterns: Using generic or duplicate title tags such as Home or Page across multiple pages
Blocking CSS and JavaScript files in robots.txt which prevents proper page rendering
Creating URLs with query parameters instead of descriptive, keyword-rich path segments

faq.question: How many title tag characters should I use?
faq.answer: Keep title tags between 50 and 60 characters. Google truncates titles longer than approximately 60 characters on desktop and mobile search results.

faq.question: Do I need a sitemap for my website?
faq.answer: Yes. A sitemap helps search engines discover and index all your important pages. Include only canonical, indexable URLs and update the lastmod date when content changes.

faq.question: What is structured data and why does it matter?
faq.answer: Structured data is JSON-LD code that tells search engines about your content. It enables rich results like star ratings, product prices, and FAQ sections in Google search results.

faq.question: Should I use noindex on my pages?
faq.answer: Only use noindex on pages you do not want in search results, such as admin pages, thank you pages, or duplicate content. Never noindex important landing pages or blog posts.

faq.question: How do I optimize images for search engines?
faq.answer: Use descriptive filenames, write specific alt text that describes the image content, compress images to reduce file size, and use modern formats like WebP with lazy loading.

faq.question: What are Core Web Vitals and do they affect SEO?
faq.answer: Core Web Vitals measure page experience with metrics like loading speed, interactivity, and visual stability. They are a confirmed Google ranking factor and account for approximately ten percent of search ranking influence.
